 {"id":"bd-ze9x","title":"bd-h5x subtask: pthread_getcpuclockid native errno + host-thread registration cleanup","notes":"Scope narrowed after investigation: fix pthread_getcpuclockid native errno mapping and improve host-backed pthread_create registration visibility for immediate introspection/cancel consumers. pthread_gettid_np still shows an older self-path SIGSEGV in this tree and is not being reclassified in support_matrix by this bead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:40:23.502673224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:00:21.770205157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T22:00:21.768186977Z","close_reason":"Implemented pthread_getcpuclockid native errno mapping and host-thread registration wait; verified with rch fmt/check/clippy and exact pthread_getcpuclockid tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ze9x","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:40:31.069623559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-zet4","title":"resolv: separate tested host-parity paths from intentional safe divergences in async-DNS stubs","description":"The async-DNS wrapper surface now mixes true host-parity cases with intentional non-crashing safety divergences. Add focused notes/tests separating the proven parity paths from the documented divergence boundaries to keep future resolver cleanup coherent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:50:26.246011442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:30:35.315223647Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:30:35.312899598Z","close_reason":"Documented and tested the split between host-parity degenerate async-DNS cases and intentional safe-divergence boundaries.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-zgifl","title":"[HIGH] cross-allocator-free pattern: 30+ raw_alloc sites return buffers callers libc::free (bd-dqqh1 cluster)","description":"## Pattern audit (bd-dqqh1 generalization)

Following the bd-dqqh1 root cause (host2netname allocates via internal arena, caller frees via libc::free → free(): invalid size SIGABRT), grep for the pattern `crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc` across crates/frankenlibc-abi/src returns 30+ call sites. Each one returns a heap buffer to its caller. If the caller is C user code under LD_PRELOAD, our interposed free() catches it correctly. If the caller is a Rust test binary (debug build, no preload), libc::free binds to glibc's free at link time and the cross-allocator free aborts with the same fingerprint.

## Confirmed sites (free-by-caller pattern, POSIX 'caller must free')

inet_abi.rs:382, 393   - getifaddrs / gethostbyname pattern
dirent_abi.rs:524, 556, 567 - scandir family
stdlib_abi.rs:4026     - realpath et al
rpc_abi.rs:666, 667, 1091, 1151, 1228, 1293 - XDR/RPC strdup-likes
rpc_abi.rs:2995        - host2netdomain (sibling of host2netname, same shape)
wchar_abi.rs:76, 1315, 4365 - wcsdup et al
unistd_abi.rs:3335, 3544, 4907, 4927, 6985, 7002, 7026, 7046,
              7218, 7224, 9868, 10097, 16246
                       - getcwd / wordexp / getifaddrs / strdup-likes
string_abi.rs:3484     - strdup probably

Each site allocates with raw_alloc (our internal arena) and the caller is expected to free via the standard free(). This works under LD_PRELOAD (our interposed free routes correctly) but fails on debug-test builds where libc::free binds to glibc's free.

## Bead bd-dqqh1 fix shape

`let buf = libc::malloc(name.len()) as *mut c_char;` instead of `raw_alloc(name.len())`. Under LD_PRELOAD libc::malloc IS our interposed malloc, so functionality is preserved. Under non-preload (test context) libc::malloc is glibc's, which pairs correctly with libc::free.

## Sweep recommendation

For each call site listed above:

1. Confirm the buffer is returned to a CALLER (vs freed in same module).
2. Confirm the contract is 'caller must free()' (POSIX docs / glibc man pages).
3. Replace raw_alloc with libc::malloc.
4. Add a regression test that calls the function under cargo test default parallelism, frees with libc::free.

Rough scope: ~30 mechanical edits + ~30 regression tests. Could be split per-module: rpc (5 callsites), unistd (13), wchar (3), inet (2), dirent (3), stdlib (1), string (1).

## Provenance

bd-dqqh1's host2netname fix at HEAD post-17431c7a fixed one site of the pattern. This umbrella bead tracks the remaining 30+. The bd-ye0f4 cluster (a581f0ac, e289f838, b04d7238 partial fixes) was likely chasing different facets of the SAME root cause.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:57:08.590918911Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:57:08.590918911Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","allocator","cross-allocator","full-review","heap","malloc","umbrella"]}

The bd-ye0f4 cluster (a581f0ac, e289f838, b04d7238 partial fixes) was likely chasing different facets of the SAME root cause.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-22T22:57:08.590918911Z", "created_by": "cc-libc", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T02:25:00.000000000Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["abi", "allocator", "cross-allocator", "full-review", "heap", "malloc", "umbrella"], "closed_at": "2026-04-23T02:25:00.000000000Z", "closed_by": "cc-libc", "notes": "Closed via cumulative drain across the session. Caller-freeable sites converted to libc::malloc/free in: 322b1254 (scandirat + IDNA + argz family), 82d9bf1f (scandir/realpath/user2netname), 61d71aa3 (strdup/strndup/wcsdup), 25ccfd15 (get_current_dir_name/tempnam), eeb9d2a7 (host2netname/user2netname), d503e6ce (xdr_bytes/string/array/reference). Remaining raw_alloc sites are internal-paired (wordexp/wordfree, glob/globfree, ftw/nftw, getifaddrs/freeifaddrs, if_nameindex/if_freenameindex, xdrrec_create/destroy) — verified safe by audit. Underlying root cause (cross-thread reentry guard race) was fixed by bd-wbqeo (6c60fbf7) which converted NATIVE_*_REENTRY guards to thread_local Cell<bool>."}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y","title":"EPIC: Eliminate all remaining host delegations (resolve_host_symbol_raw to 0)","description":"## Goal
Eliminate every remaining resolve_host_symbol_raw call across the ABI crate (except host_resolve.rs which IS the resolver infrastructure), bringing FrankenLibC to zero host libc runtime dependencies for its classified symbol surface.

## Background
As of commit d974b43, 64 host delegations were eliminated (57 from unistd_abi.rs, 6 from rpc_abi.rs, 1 from glibc_internal_abi.rs). Code audit reveals 31 remaining calls across 7 files (excluding host_resolve.rs):

### Remaining by file (31 total, verified by code audit)
| File | Calls | Unique Symbols | Category |
|------|-------|---------------|----------|
| io_internal_abi.rs | 7 | 3 (_IO_list_all, _IO_file_jumps, _IO_wfile_jumps) | glibc stream internals |
| setjmp_abi.rs | 6 | 4 (setjmp, _setjmp, __sigsetjmp, longjmp/siglongjmp) | register save/restore |
| stdio_abi.rs | 5 | 5 (stdin/stdout/stderr, setvbuf, host fn cache) | host FILE* bootstrap |
| pthread_abi.rs | 5 | 5 (getattr_np, cond_clockwait, timedjoin, tryjoin, clockjoin) | pthread extensions |
| dlfcn_abi.rs | 4 | 3 (dlopen x2, dlsym, dlclose) | dynamic loader |
| stdlib_abi.rs | 3 | 3 (setenv, unsetenv, putenv) | environ array |
| resolv_abi.rs | 2 | 2 (getaddrinfo, freeaddrinfo) | DNS resolution |

Note: host_resolve.rs has 3 calls but is the resolver infrastructure itself — not a promotion target.

### Two categories of remaining delegations
**Category A — Intentional Interpose Delegations (18 calls):**
These delegate to host ONLY for objects the host created (non-managed). Native paths already work for FrankenLibC-managed objects. Correct for LD_PRELOAD mode; dead code in replace mode.
- pthread_abi.rs: 5 (all conditional on !is_managed_*)
- dlfcn_abi.rs: 3 (dlsym/dlclose for host-opened handles)
- stdio_abi.rs: 2 (setvbuf + host fn cache for non-registry FILE*)
- stdlib_abi.rs: 3 (prefer host to avoid environ ownership conflict)

**Category B — Genuinely Missing Native Implementations (13 calls):**
These need new code — the host delegation is the primary/only path.
- setjmp_abi.rs: 6 (UNCONDITIONAL — always delegates, needs assembly)
- io_internal_abi.rs: 7 (bootstrap host glibc _IO_* structures)
- stdio_abi.rs: 3 (stdin/stdout/stderr global resolution)
- resolv_abi.rs: 1 (getaddrinfo DNS fallback — /etc/hosts already native)
- dlfcn_abi.rs: 1 (dlopen bootstrap passthrough)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero resolve_host_symbol_raw calls in ABI crate except host_resolve.rs
- All existing tests pass (156 lib + 113 unistd + 88 stdio + 35 rpc + 21 dlfcn + 21 setjmp + 163 pthread + all others)
- Full workspace clippy clean
- Each sub-bead includes: unit tests, e2e script, structured logging, verification commands
- Category A beads may be deferred to replace-mode milestone if interpose behavior is correct","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:41:10.103151134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:09.722735542Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:09.722366513Z","close_reason":"ALL 7 children closed: bd-zh1y.1 (pthread extensions), bd-zh1y.2 (setjmp/longjmp asm), bd-zh1y.3 (native FILE/vtable/registry), bd-zh1y.4 (stdio globals), bd-zh1y.5 (dlfcn classification), bd-zh1y.6 (environ ownership), bd-zh1y.7 (getaddrinfo DNS). Host delegations reduced from ~102 to ~8 (5 intentional dlfcn interpose + 3 host_resolve infrastructure). LD_PRELOAD smoke: 58/0/6.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","host-delegation","native"],"comments":[{"id":1344,"issue_id":"bd-zh1y","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"Created by ChartreuseCrane after eliminating 64 host delegations (57 unistd, 6 rpc, 1 glibc_internal). This epic tracks the remaining 35 (38 minus 3 in host_resolve.rs which must stay). Subtasks ordered by difficulty: pthread (MEDIUM, most independent), setjmp (HARD, assembly), io_internal (HARD, architecture), stdio (MEDIUM, blocked on io_internal), dlfcn (HARD, loader), stdlib (MEDIUM, startup), resolv (HARD, DNS). Recommended execution: pthread first, then stdlib, then setjmp, then io_internal+stdio together, then resolv, then dlfcn last.","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:15Z"},{"id":1346,"issue_id":"bd-zh1y","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"KEY INSIGHT (ChartreuseCrane): After deep code analysis, the remaining host delegations fall into TWO categories: (1) INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE DELEGATIONS — delegate to host for objects the host created (pthread condvars/threads/mutexes, dlopen handles, stdin/stdout FILE*). These are CORRECT for LD_PRELOAD mode and would be dead code in replace mode. Includes: 5 pthread, 5 dlfcn, 5 stdio, 3 stdlib. (2) GENUINELY MISSING native implementations — require new code to eliminate. Includes: 7 setjmp (assembly), 8 io_internal (native FILE struct), 2 resolv (DNS). The epic should track both, but priority should be on category (2) since category (1) is correct interpose behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:48:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.1","title":"Promote pthread extension APIs to native futex/syscall (5 delegations)","description":"## Goal
Classify 5 pthread host delegations as intentional interpose behavior and ensure native paths are robust.

## Classification: INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE DELEGATIONS
All 5 delegations are CONDITIONAL — they only fire for non-managed (glibc-created) objects. For FrankenLibC-managed objects, native paths already exist and work via futex/syscall. This is correct LD_PRELOAD behavior.

### Current code structure (verified by audit)
1. pthread_getattr_np (line 3658): delegates for non-managed threads; native path reads /proc/self/task/[tid] for managed threads
2. pthread_cond_clockwait (line 5185): delegates for non-managed condvars; native path uses futex with clock conversion for managed condvars
3. pthread_timedjoin_np (line 5237): delegates for non-managed threads; native path uses futex-based timed wait for managed threads
4. pthread_tryjoin_np (line 5332): delegates for non-managed threads; native path checks managed thread state atomically
5. pthread_clockjoin_np (line 5401): delegates for non-managed threads; native path converts clock and delegates to timedjoin

### What needs to happen
Phase 1 (this bead): Verify native paths are complete and correct. Add comprehensive tests. Add FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING/FORCE_NATIVE_MUTEX e2e coverage. Document the intentional delegation pattern.
Phase 2 (replace-mode milestone): When FrankenLibC owns all thread creation, non-managed paths become dead code. Remove host delegation at that point.

## Implementation tasks
1. Add unit tests for each extension API on managed objects (native path exercised)
2. Add e2e test script that sets FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING=1 and exercises all 5 APIs without host delegation
3. Add a guard assertion: if FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING is set, resolve_host_symbol_raw must never be reached (add debug_assert or metric)
4. Update support_matrix classification for these 5 symbols to Implemented (native path) with interpose note

## Testing requirements
- Unit tests: nominal + edge cases for each API (timeout=0, timeout=past, invalid args, EINVAL paths)
- e2e script: scripts/check_pthread_extensions_native.sh exercising all 5 with FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING=1
- Structured log fields: trace_id, api_family=pthread, symbol, mode (native vs host), decision_path, errno, latency_ns
- Verify: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test (all 163 tests pass)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.015769400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T21:04:43.801009502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T21:04:43.800725133Z","close_reason":"All 4 child beads closed: bd-zh1y.1.1 (getattr_np /proc maps), bd-zh1y.1.2 (cond_clockwait futex), bd-zh1y.1.3 (tryjoin/timedjoin native), bd-zh1y.1.4 (clockjoin native). All 5 pthread extension APIs have dual native+host paths with FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING test coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["host-delegation","native","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.1","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.015769400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1345,"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.1","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"ANALYSIS UPDATE (ChartreuseCrane): After reading the code, all 5 pthread host delegations are INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE DELEGATIONS, not missing implementations. They only fire for non-managed (glibc-created) objects. For FrankenLibC-managed objects, native paths already exist and work. In replace mode (future), these would be dead code since all objects would be managed. For interpose mode, delegation for non-managed objects is CORRECT. Subtasks should be reclassified: .1.1 getattr_np is the only one that could benefit from improvement (eliminating the populate_managed_attr_from_host_attr translation step). The others (.1.2-.1.4) are already correctly implemented for managed objects.","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:47:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.1.1","title":"Native pthread_getattr_np via /proc/self/task maps parsing","description":"Implement pthread_getattr_np by reading /proc/self/task/[tid]/maps to find stack boundaries, combining with thread-local attribute state (detach state, guard size, stack size). This is what glibc does internally. Must handle main thread (stack at top of address space) vs spawned threads (stack allocated by pthread_create).","acceptance_criteria":"pthread_getattr_np returns correct stack base/size and detach state for managed threads. Unit tests: spawn thread, call getattr_np, verify stack size >= PTHREAD_STACK_MIN, verify detach state matches creation args. e2e: scripts/check_pthread_extensions_native.sh with FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING=1.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:46:09.218021356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:44:50.146623867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:44:50.146249178Z","close_reason":"Implemented stack_bounds_from_proc_maps() fallback in pthread_getattr_np. Parses /proc/self/task/<tid>/maps for [stack] annotation (main thread) and SP containment (spawned threads). Added 2 tests: main thread and std::thread via /proc maps with FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING. All 6 getattr_np tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:46:09.218021356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
